Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions

Class Tactics

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.Tactics
public class Tactics extends Object
This class contains static methods that wrap Event-B reasoner extensions into tactics. In many cases, applicability methods are also included that implement a quick check to see if the tactic may be applicable in a particular situation.
Since:
1.0

    allmpD(Predicate univHyp, String... instantiations)
    Tactic for instantiating a univaersally quantified implicative hypothesis and performing a modus ponens on it in one step.
    static boolean
    allmpD_applicable(Predicate hyp)
    Tells if it is possible to instantiate the given hypothesis and further apply modus ponens, or modus tollens on it in one step.
    static ITactic
    allmtD(Predicate univHyp, String... instantiations)
    Tactic for instantiating a universally quantified implicative hypothesis and performing a modus tollens on it in one step.

    • Tactics

      public Tactics()

  • Method Details

    • review

      public static ITactic review(int reviewerConfidence)
      The review tactic.
      Parameters:
      reviewerConfidence - The reviewer confidence to use.
      Returns:
      The resulting tactic

    • lemma

      public static ITactic lemma(String lemma)
      The add lemma tactic. Introduces a lemma (and its well definedness condition) at a given open proof tree node.
      Parameters:
      lemma - The lemma to introduce as a String.
      Returns:
      The resulting lemma tactic.

    • insertLemma

      public static ITactic insertLemma(String lemma)
      The insert lemma tactic. Inserts a lemma (and its well definedness condition) at a given proof tree node. This proof tree node need not be open.
      Parameters:
      lemma - The lemma to insert as a String.
      Returns:
      The resulting tactic.

    • abstrExpr

      public static ITactic abstrExpr(String expression)
      The abstract expression tactic. Abstracts an expression (and introduces its well definedness condition) into a proof.
      Parameters:
      expression - The expression to abstract as a String.
      Returns:
      The resulting Abstract Expression tactic.

    • abstrExprThenEq

      public static ITactic abstrExprThenEq(String expression)

    • abstrExprThenEqL0

      public static ITactic abstrExprThenEqL0(String expression)
      Old version of abstrExprThenEq(String) that uses the level 0 of Eq. This should not be used in new code. It is only used for backwards compatibility in old tests.
      Parameters:
      expression - expression on which the tactics are applied
      Returns:
      tactics ae then eq
      Since:
      3.6

    • abstrExprThenEq

      private static ITactic abstrExprThenEq(String expression, EqHe.Level level)

    • getLastAddedHyp

      private static Predicate getLastAddedHyp(IProofRule.IAntecedent antecedent)
      Returns the last added hypothesis of the given antecedent, or null if the antecedent contains no added hypotheses.
      Parameters:
      antecedent - the given antecedent
      Returns:
      the last added hypothesis of the given antecedent, or null if the antecedent contains no added hypotheses.

    • doCase

      public static ITactic doCase(String trueCase)
      The do case tactic. Introduces a case distinction on a predicate into a proof.
      Parameters:
      trueCase - The true case of the case distinction as a String.
      Returns:
      The resulting do case tactic.

    • lasoo

      public static ITactic lasoo()
      The lasoo tactic. This tactic selects all hypotheses that have free identifiers in common with the current goal and currently selected hypotheses.
      Returns:
      The lasoo tactic.

    • removeHiddenAndSelectedHyps

      private static void removeHiddenAndSelectedHyps(Set<Predicate> hyps, IProverSequent sequent)

    • autoRewrite

      public static ITactic autoRewrite()
      Runs the auto formula rewriter that normalizes all predicates of the sequent at the given node.
      Since:
      2.3

    • allI

      public static ITactic allI(String input)
      Returns the tactic for AllI with an input. The input should be the user-provided, comma-separated list of identifiers to use to generate fresh names for the bound identifiers.
      Parameters:
      input - user input
      Returns:
      the tactic for the introduction of universal quantification
      Since:
      3.7

      public static ITactic allmpD(Predicate univHyp, String... instantiations)
      Tactic for instantiating a univaersally quantified implicative hypothesis and performing a modus ponens on it in one step.
      Parameters:
      univHyp -
      instantiations -
      Returns:
      the tactic

    • allmtD

      public static ITactic allmtD(Predicate univHyp, String... instantiations)
      Tactic for instantiating a universally quantified implicative hypothesis and performing a modus tollens on it in one step.
      Parameters:
      univHyp -
      instantiations -
      Returns:
      the tactic
      Since:
      2.2

    • allD_applicable

      public static boolean allD_applicable(Predicate hyp)

    • allmpD_applicable

      public static boolean allmpD_applicable(Predicate hyp)
      Tells if it is possible to instantiate the given hypothesis and further apply modus ponens, or modus tollens on it in one step.
      Parameters:
      hyp - the hypothesis on which we want to apply the tactic
      Returns:
      true if the tactics allmpD and allmtD are applicable on the selected hypothesis FIXME change the name of this method as it is now also used for the modus tollens

    • exF

      public static ITactic exF(Predicate exHyp, String input)
      Returns the "free existential" tactic for a given hypothesis and user input. The input should be the user-provided, comma-separated list of identifiers to use to generate fresh names for bound identifiers that will be freed.
      Parameters:
      exHyp - the hypothesis on which the tactic will apply
      input - user input
      Returns:
      the tactic "free existential"
      Since:
      3.7

    • contImpHyp

      public static ITactic contImpHyp(Predicate hyp, IPosition position)
      Returns a tactic to rewrite an implicative sub-predicate, occurring in an hypothesis, to its contrapositive.
      Parameters:
      hyp - an hypothesis predicate that contains the sub-predicate to rewrite
      position - position of the sub-predicate to rewrite
      Returns:
      a tactic to rewrite an implicative sub-predicate to its contrapositive

    • getArithRewriter

      public static IFormulaRewriter getArithRewriter()

      the arithmetic rewriter
      Since:
      3.0

    • isOnePointApplicable

      public static boolean isOnePointApplicable(Predicate predicate)
      Returns whether the one-point rule is applicable to the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate to check
      Returns:
      true iff one-point rules is applicable to the given predicate
      Since:
      3.0

    • onePointGoal

      public static ITactic onePointGoal()
      Returns the tactic "one-point rule" OnePointRule which is applicable to the goal of a sequent.
      Returns:
      the tactic "one-point rule on goal"
      Since:
      1.1

    • onePointHyp

      public static ITactic onePointHyp(Predicate hyp)
      Returns the tactic "one-point rule" OnePointRule which is applicable to a hypothesis of a sequent.
      Parameters:
      hyp - a hypothesis on which one-point rule is applicable
      Returns:
      the tactic "one-point rule on hyp"
      Since:
      1.1

    • totalDomRewrites

      public static ITactic totalDomRewrites(Predicate hyp, IPosition position, Expression substitute)
      Returns the tactic "total domain substitution" for a given substitute. It is applicable to any predicate of a sequent.
      Parameters:
      hyp - a hypothesis, or null to specify the goal
      position - a valid position of an expression in the specified predicate
      substitute - a substitute to the specified expression
      Returns:
      the tactic "total domain substitution"
      Since:
      1.1

    • totalDomGetSubstitutions

      public static Set<Expression> totalDomGetSubstitutions(IProverSequent sequent, Expression expression)
      Returns a set of possible total domain substitutions for the given expression in the given sequent.
      Parameters:
      sequent - a sequent
      expression - an expression to substitute
      Returns:
      a set of substitutes (empty if none was found)
      Since:
      1.1

    • funImgSimplifies

      public static ITactic funImgSimplifies(Predicate hyp, IPosition position)
      Returns the tactic "Functional Image Simplification" for a given position where this tactic can apply.
      Parameters:
      hyp - the hypothesis to rewrite or null if the goal shall be rewritten
      position - a valid position of an expression in the goal or hypothesis
      Returns:
      the tactic "Functional Image Simplification"
      Since:
      2.0

    • funImgSimpGetPositions

      public static List<IPosition> funImgSimpGetPositions(Predicate hyp, IProverSequent sequent)
      Returns a set of positions where the rewriter funImgSimpRewrites can apply.
      Parameters:
      hyp - the hypothesis to rewrite or null if the goal shall be rewritten
      sequent - the current sequent
      Returns:
      a set of positions (empty if the tactic is not applicable)
      Since:
      2.0

    • isFunImgSimpApplicable

      public static boolean isFunImgSimpApplicable(Expression funImgExpr, IProverSequent sequent)
      Tells if the tactic funImgSimpRewrites can apply.
      Parameters:
      funImgExpr - the expression to rewrite. It is a functional image expression where the tactic funImgSimpRewrites could apply
      sequent - the current sequent
      Returns:
      true if the tactic is not applicable, false otherwise
      Since:
      2.0

    • funImgGoal

      public static ITactic funImgGoal(Predicate hyp, IPosition position)
      Returns the tactic "Functional Image Goal" for a given position where this tactic can apply.
      Parameters:
      hyp - the hypothesis of the form f ∈ S1 op S2
      position - a valid position of an function application f(E) in the goal
      Returns:
      the tactic "Functional Image Goal"
      Since:
      2.0

    • dtDCInducGetPositions

      @Deprecated public static List<IPosition> dtDCInducGetPositions(Predicate predicate)
      Deprecated.
      use either dtDCGetPositions(Predicate) or dtInducGetPositions(Predicate)
      Returns the list of applicable positions of the tactic "Datatype Distinct Case" DTDistinctCase to the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate
      Returns:
      a list of positions (empty if the tactic is not applicable)
      Since:
      2.0

    • dtDCGetPositions

      public static List<IPosition> dtDCGetPositions(Predicate predicate)
      Returns the list of applicable positions of the tactic "Datatype Distinct Case" DTDistinctCase to the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate
      Returns:
      a list of positions (empty if the tactic is not applicable)
      Since:
      3.6

    • dtInducGetPositions

      public static List<IPosition> dtInducGetPositions(Predicate predicate)
      Determines if the tactic "Datatype Induction" DTInduction is applicable to the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      predicate - a predicate
      Returns:
      whether the tactic is applicable
      Since:
      3.6

    • dtDistinctCase

      public static ITactic dtDistinctCase(Predicate hyp, IPosition position)
      Returns the tactic "Datatype Distinct Case" for a given position where this tactic can be applied.
      Parameters:
      hyp - a hypothesis or null if the application happens in goal
      position - the position of the application
      Returns:
      the tactic "Datatype Distinct Case"
      Since:
      2.0

    • dtDistinctCase

      public static ITactic dtDistinctCase(Predicate hyp, IPosition position, String input)
      Returns the tactic "Datatype Distinct Case" for a given position where this tactic can be applied, with an input. The input should be the user-provided, comma-separated list of identifiers to use to generate fresh names for the constructor parameters.
      Parameters:
      hyp - a hypothesis or null if the application happens in goal
      position - the position of the application
      input - user input
      Returns:
      the tactic "Datatype Distinct Case"
      Since:
      3.7

    • dtInduction

      @Deprecated public static ITactic dtInduction(Predicate hyp, IPosition position)
      Deprecated.
      use dtInduction(IPosition)
      Returns the tactic "Datatype Induction" for a given position where this tactic can be applied.
      Parameters:
      hyp - a hypothesis or null if the application happens in goal
      position - the position of the application
      Returns:
      the tactic "Datatype Induction"
      Since:
      2.0

    • dtInduction

      public static ITactic dtInduction(IPosition position)
      Returns the tactic "Datatype Induction".
      Returns:
      the tactic "Datatype Induction"
      Since:
      3.6

      public static ITactic cardDef(Predicate hyp, IPosition position)
      Returns the tactic for the CardDefRewrites reasoner for a given position where it can be applied.
      Parameters:
      hyp - a hypothesis or null if the application is in goal
      position - the position of the application
      Returns:
      the tactic "Cardinal definition"
      Since:
      3.6

      Returns the list of applicable positions of the reasoner CardDefRewrites to a predicate.

      Returns:
      a list of applicable positions
      Since:
